name: Lakshmi Puja Sankalp
duration_note: Performed as a full puja session (1–2 hours). Most powerful on Diwali night, Kojagari Purnima, and Fridays.

recurrence.repeat_note: Most traditional: every Friday for 16 consecutive Fridays (Solah Shukrawar Vrat). Also performed on each Purnima. Or once a year on Diwali as the grand family puja.

sankalpa_text_template: ममात्मनः परिवारस्य च समृद्ध्यर्थं धनधान्यसम्पदप्राप्त्यर्थं श्रीमहालक्ष्मीप्रसादसिद्ध्यर्थं श्रीमहालक्ष्मीपूजनं करिष्ये ।
sankalpa_guide: Face east. Hold flowers and water. Say your name, gotra, and family members you include in this puja. Recite the sankalpa. Release the water.

preparation: Clean the house thoroughly before Lakshmi Puja — Lakshmi does not enter dirty or cluttered homes
Decorate the entrance with rangoli and flowers — especially lotus motifs
Light ghee lamps in every room of the house to invite Lakshmi
If fasting: take only one meal before the puja (fruit and milk) and fast after the puja
For Friday Vrat: observe every Friday for 16 consecutive weeks. Keep track in the app.

materials.name: Lotus flowers (or lotus-shaped items)
materials.purpose: Lakshmi's seat and most beloved flower

materials.name: Gold or silver coin
materials.purpose: Placed before Lakshmi during puja — sanctified and kept in the home after
materials.substitute: Any coin — the act of placing it matters more than its value

materials.name: Shri Yantra (if available)
materials.purpose: The geometric form of Lakshmi's energy — placing it on the altar focuses her grace

materials.name: Rice
materials.purpose: Placed under the Lakshmi image — rice is Lakshmi's grain (Dhaanya Lakshmi — Lakshmi as grain)

materials.name: Turmeric (haldi)
materials.purpose: Lakshmi is associated with gold — turmeric's gold color is her symbol

materials.name: Red vermilion (kumkum)
materials.purpose: Applied to Lakshmi's forehead and offered
materials.substitute: None — kumkum is essential for Devi worship

materials.name: Ghee lamp
materials.quantity: Multiple — one in each room is ideal
materials.purpose: Inviting Lakshmi through light into every room of the house

materials.name: Incense (sandalwood or jasmine)
materials.purpose: Fragrance offering to Lakshmi — she is said to be pleased by sweet fragrances

materials.name: Kheer (rice pudding) or Panjiri
materials.purpose: Lakshmi's naivedya — sweet offerings for the goddess of abundance

steps.title: Purify and Set Up
steps.action: Bathe. Light ghee lamps throughout the house first. Then set up the altar: yellow/gold cloth, Lakshmi's image/idol, the gold/silver coin, the Shri Yantra if available. Place rice underneath the image.
steps.note: The lights throughout the house are part of the puja — they are invitations to Lakshmi to enter every room.

steps.title: Ganesha Vandana (First)
steps.action: Always begin with Ganesha — the remover of obstacles that would interrupt the puja.

steps.title: Sankalpa
steps.action: Hold water and flowers. Recite the Lakshmi Puja Sankalpa — name your family members explicitly in the sankalpa — this puja's abundance is for the whole family.

steps.title: Invoke Lakshmi — Shri Suktam
steps.action: Chant the 6 key verses of Shri Suktam. This is the heart of the puja. Chant with full attention, imagining Lakshmi arriving at your door as a beloved guest.
steps.note: If you know all 15 verses of Shri Suktam, recite all 15. Even just verses 1 and 5 (golden Lakshmi + removing Alakshmi) are powerful.

steps.title: Offer 16 items (Shodashopachara)
steps.action: Offer each puja item to Lakshmi: flowers, incense, lamp, food, water, kumkum, turmeric, sandalwood paste, cloth, ornaments. With each offering, recite 'Om Mahalaksmyai Namah'.
steps.note: If short on time: the 5 essential offerings are light (diya), flowers, incense, kumkum, and naivedya (kheer). These 5 alone are complete.

steps.title: Chant Mahalakshmi Ashtakam
steps.action: Recite all 8 verses of the Mahalakshmi Ashtakam. Each verse names a different power of the Goddess.

steps.title: Lakshmi Gayatri — 108 repetitions
steps.action: Sit quietly with a mala. Chant the Mahalakshmi Gayatri 108 times — this is the meditative core of the puja.
steps.note: This is the closest thing to Lakshmi Dhyan (meditation). Eyes closed, focus on the image of Lakshmi as golden light entering your home and your heart.

steps.title: Aarti — Jay Mahalakshmi Mata
steps.action: Wave the ghee lamp in circles before Lakshmi while singing aarti. Ring the bell.

steps.title: Distribute Prasad
steps.action: Offer the kheer or sweet to Lakshmi. Then distribute to all family. If possible, leave some prasad outside the house — for any passerby — this is charity in Lakshmi's name.
steps.note: Lakshmi multiplies through sharing. Keeping all prasad for yourself without sharing reduces her flow.

steps.title: Kshama Prarthana
steps.action: Conclude with the forgiveness prayer for any errors.

mantras_explained.why_this_mantra: Shri Suktam is from the Rigveda — among the oldest hymns in any living tradition. It calls Lakshmi through fire (Agni) as messenger, describes her as golden, lotus-seated, and compassionate. Verse 5 explicitly asks to remove 'Alakshmi' — the energy of poverty and strife. This is pragmatic spirituality: name what you want to leave, name what you want to arrive.
mantras_explained.how_to_pronounce: Hi-ran-ya-var-naam Ha-ri-neem Su-var-na-ra-ja-ta-sra-jaam | Chan-draam Hi-ran-ma-yeem Lak-shmeem Jaa-ta-ve-do Ma Aa-va-ha ||
mantras_explained.what_to_visualize: The goddess arriving — preceded by elephants pouring water, in the center of a chariot, draped in gold. She arrives at YOUR door. She steps inside your home. She looks around: is this a worthy home? And you show her: yes, it is clean, lit, and fragrant. She smiles and sits.

mantras_explained.why_this_mantra: The Gayatri form of Lakshmi worship is not a petition — it is a meditation. 'We know her... we meditate on her... may she enlighten us.' This moves beyond asking for money to seeking the wisdom that creates abundance. Lakshmi is not just material prosperity — she is also Vidya Lakshmi (wisdom), Dhairya Lakshmi (courage), Vijaya Lakshmi (victory). The Gayatri connects you to all 8 forms at once.
mantras_explained.how_to_pronounce: Om Ma-haa-Lak-shmyai Cha Vid-ma-he Vish-nu-pat-nyai Cha Dhee-ma-hi | Tan-no Lak-shmih Pra-cho-da-yaat ||
mantras_explained.what_to_visualize: Lakshmi seated on a lotus in a still lake. The lake is your mind — when it becomes still and clean, the lotus of Lakshmi can bloom in it. Each repetition of the Gayatri is one ripple across the lake's surface — until the water becomes mirror-still.

alarms.label: Friday — Lakshmi Vrat Day
alarms.notification_message: Shukravar (Friday) — Lakshmi Puja day. Fast and prepare for evening puja.

alarms.label: Evening Lakshmi Puja — Light All Lamps
alarms.notification_message: Light lamps in all rooms now — Lakshmi Puja begins at sunset

materials_disposal.disposal_method: KEEP — do not throw. Place in your wallet, safe, or keep on the altar permanently.
materials_disposal.note: This coin has received Lakshmi's blessing during the puja — it becomes a permanent token of her presence in your home

materials_disposal.disposal_method: Cook and eat this rice — it is Dhaanya Lakshmi prasad (grain blessing)
Immerse in water or give to a garden
Distribute to all family and neighbors. Send some to the poor.
materials_disposal.note: Annadaan (food charity) in Lakshmi's name multiplies her grace

after_completion.prasad_distribution: Distribute kheer to all family first. Then send some to neighbors. Leave a portion outside or give to anyone who passes by. Lakshmi flows through generosity.
next_steps: If doing Solah Shukrawar Vrat: mark this Friday as complete. Count: this is Friday [X] of 16.
The 16th (final) Friday requires a larger puja with Brahmin dakshina and broader prasad distribution
Keep all lamps burning as long as possible on puja night — do not rush to extinguish them

udyapan.description: Solah Shukrawar Vrat (16 Fridays) has a mandatory Udyapan on the 16th Friday. This includes: expanded puja with full Shri Suktam, invitation of female relatives for blessings, offering gold ornaments to a married woman (Suhagan), and distributing prasad widely. The 16th Friday puja is meant to be the biggest of the 16.
